hints.text: Plates **white or yellow** (yellow rarer); taxis: **yellow top / white bottom**

shared_with.note: Yellow plates in S. America: only Peru, Colombia (+ rarely Chile).

hints.text: Gen3: solid **black or white** Google car; **Peru HAS Gen4** (Bolivia none; Ecuador's Gen4 = big pickup); smallcam exists

shared_with.note: Black car also AR/UY; smallcam also AR/BR.
shared_with.diff: B&W km markers + striped posts â†’ Peru.

shared_with.note: White car too â€” but NO Gen4 at all in Bolivia.

shared_with.diff: Chile's black-section variant; striped posts â†’ Peru.

hints.text: Always **white outer lines**; highways: single dashed or double yellow middle; **rural mountain roads: white outers, NO centre line** (in S. America predominantly Peru); divided highways can be all-white
**Black-and-white striped signposts** â€” the only South American country (some areas black-yellow); metal signposts sit in **concrete blocks** (also S.America-unique)
Bollards red or yellow, mostly **triangular concrete** (circular variants exist)
Guardrails w/ **black-yellow stripes** (sometimes arrow design)

shared_with.diff: Outer stripe: **black â†’ Peru, yellow â†’ Bolivia**.

hints.text: km markers: **concrete, painted black & white** (road number near urban areas)
Poles concrete or wood, sometimes w/ **horizontal concrete bars** (also Argentina); painted bottoms **black or black-yellow** (S.America-unique pattern; Colombia similar)
Bridges commonly painted **orange**; landscape default = **semi-arid mountain ranges & valleys**
**Mototaxis** (tuk-tuks) everywhere (Lima, Trujillo, Chiclayo, Piura, Iquitos); taxis w/ wide **red-white side stickers**
Electricity counters: **rectangular, metallic, 2â€“3 horizontal slits** â€” unique design
Currency **Sol (S/.)** on price signs; many **political-party logos** painted on walls â€” all Peru-unique
Architecture mix: colonial buildings, contemporary 'boxy' concrete, traditional **red brick**

Coverage mostly **west + main highways** (exceptions: around Iquitos + the 30C east); terrain: **mountainous west, flat east** (but flat desert near Piura NW); climate: desert west, rainforest east; highway numbers rise **northâ†’south** (1/3/5 = the long ones)
**Landscape & flora by region**:
bullets: Tumbes off-roads: sandy soil + green/withered bushes â€” and NEVER a black car (white only)
SW-Piura: dry flat w/ few shrubs; NW of Piura city: same but hillier
Coconut/tall palms: coastal agriculture north of Lima
Sugar cane: north coast near Chiclayo/Trujillo; bananas similar (north coast)
Flat high-mountain plains: central Puno + around Lake Chinchaycocha (Bolivia-like!)
South of Lake Titicaca: low grass, stone walls, brown brick
Far north: shrub-covered mountains, drier toward the coast/Ecuador
Central highlands: exposed rocky outcroppings (JunĂ­nâ†’Ayacucho); sharp rocky peaks Huarazâ†’Cusco
Light-green lichen on rocks: high central/southern Peru
Dark red soil: Cusco, Ayacucho, Cajamarca, Tarapoto
Ica/Arequipa/Tacna: barren flat desert w/ mountains east (N-Chile look)
Central/south coastal farm valleys: SW-NE between bare dry mountains

hints.text: **Red brick + blue-tinted-glass buildings increase toward Lake Titicaca** (W-Bolivia look â€” caution, red brick exists everywhere); **overhanging roofs w/ wooden supports** â†’ north (often brightly coloured)

**Pole markings**: yellow vertical numbers â†’ north coast + Tacna, often w/ a horizontal 2â€“3-letter **city code** (CH = Chimbote); open-front tuk-tuks â†’ northern half; **Lima lamps = inverted-J sweep** (occasionally Iquitos/Pucallpa); cylindrical bollard w/ black-yellow top â†’ only **Moquegua**

**Road spotlights**: 1N at the Piura/Lambayeque border = semi-desert, overcast Gen3; 1N/1S divided section = the only rural divided highway; 1S south of Ica = coast-hugging, red-orange soil, all Gen4; 5N (San Alejandroâ†”Esperanza) = straight flat rainforest road; 30C (PlanchĂłnâ†”IĂ±apari) = white Gen3 car, dense forest, Brazil-interior look; 28B (Kimbiriâ†”Echarate) = faded/missing lines, hillside + river valley; Jaujaâ†”Huancayo valley = flat plain w/ trees + villages between N-S ranges

**Lakes & volcanoes**: north side of Titicaca = wetter-season coverage than the south side; Lake Chinchaycocha = marshy 'middle lake' w/ mountains east (less populated than Titicaca); **volcanoes â†’ only the southeast** (esp. around Arequipa)

**Cities**: Lima = long straight divided boulevards; Pucallpa = consistent WSW-ENE grid; **Iquitos** = isolated Gen3-only Amazon city w/ tuk-tuks + overcast; **Cusco** = red brick + colonial mix, ringed by mountains, airport mid-city (La-Paz-like!); Tacna = dry ridge to the north; Puerto Maldonado = moto-taxi drivers in **yellow vests**
**Machu Picchu**: white Street View car Aguas Calientes â†’ Machu Picchu; train-track trekker between Aguas Calientes & Cusco
